reviewSample.text:
Impressed.

Everyone says to get the tres leches latte - but that’s too much milk/sugar for me.

The barista was super friendly and helpful, she explained that the tres leches milk they actually make in-house (it’s not just syrups).

So, she made me an iced americano, and instead of me putting in some cream, she put in a little bit of the tres leches milk. WOW. Very very good, not too sweet, but you can definitely taste the sweetness and even a little coconut.

I will 100% be back for this!!

Highly recommend asking for the tres leches milk added in small part to your basic coffee order.

First time checking out this spot in the neighborhood and it did not disappoint! I had the Chai Latte and my wife got the Caramel Latte both were delicious with awesome flavor. Super friendly vibe, great service, and definitely a new go to for our weekend coffee runs!

Love this place!!! Very cozy, friendly atmosphere and coffee is exceptional! Amazing way to start your day and it is a real treat!!! Parking may be a challenge but it is so worth it. They don’t have a lot of sitting areas bec the concept is to go but we always stay and hang around. Would love for owner to add throw pillows around for clients to sit and hang around:-) but coffee is a killer and flavors are sincerely good … Must try, must go to place doesn’t matter what time of the day

reviewSample.text: Stopped by this coffee shop because we had a long drive ahead of us from Miami, FL back home, & we are coffee enthusiasts. Out of all the coffee shops I’ve been to, this is in my top two. My buddy told me this is genuine Cuban coffee, & it is! The location is amazing, & the shop is well-decorated & organized. They had multiple food items & lots of coffee options. I forgot exactly what I ordered, but it was fresh, cold, &—most of all—tasty! The staff were friendly, helpful, & professional.
I could say it really good for first time to taste Cuba coffee. I’ve told the staff that I cannot drink too sweet coffee please less stweet. Then I gotta a perfect one!!! It’s sooooo good!

I want to recognize Oscar, Dalila, and Gipsy for their excellent customer service. They are consistently friendly, attentive, and make every visit a great experience. Their professionalism truly stands out. My matcha 11/10

reviewSample.text: Living in Key West, I have been coming to Miami weekly for medical care for over a year and I discovered Navi towards the beginning of 2025 and it’s easily become one of my favorite go-to’s in Miami and a place that has made these trips worth looking forward to. Not only are their lattes delicious, they have amazing pastries and it’s always my lucky day when they have their pistachio croissants. They have a cozy and small aesthetic with fresh florals always available, but what has made this spot so special to me is the treatment from their baristas and owners. They have always been so warm and welcoming. Navi recently made floral arrangements for us to give to our surgeons and they were the most beautiful, whimsical bohemian style arrangements that I could tell were made with so much love. I will be coming here for all of my Miami floral needs from here on out. You can’t go wrong with a stop at Navi!

The coffee shop is really cute, with a cozy and nice vibe ✨. The service was great☺️, the person at the register was super friendly. I ordered a classic matcha with almond milk and cinnamon, no sweeteners. The matcha was good, though a bit bitter, it might be a mix between culinary and ceremonial grade matcha. Overall, I liked it and would come back💖. It was around $9 for the matcha. If you’re a matcha lover, I’d say it’s worth trying, even though it’s not the best I’ve ever had.

Delightful little cafe/coffee shop. I had a wonderful iced latte and the barista was so friendly and helpful when explaining the drink options. They had some tasty looking pastries, but I was in a bit of a hurry and didn’t have the opportunity to try them. Such a clean aesthetic and bright and airy interior. There is a good amount of seating outside too which is nice and most of those seats are shaded. They also sell beautiful arrangements of flowers :)

reviewSample.text: We came for breakfast on our last day in Miami. It’s a a  well decorated, cozy place with an inviting vibe.  The owner was super friendly and accommodating. I had an iced americano and a delicious blueberry danish. We sat outside and had a perfect morning.

I found this place today and honestly didn’t expect it to be that good! Omg!!! The pistachio cheesecake is the best I’ve ever had in my life. The coffee was amazing too. The owner has Italian roots, he really knows what good desserts should taste like. He’s also super nice and easy to talk to, which made the whole experience even better. Thank you! My favourite coffee spot now in Miami
P.S. The cheesecake was so delicious that I even forgot to take a photo because I started eating it right away 😂

I went to La Suiza today and enjoyed the experience and the food. I had previously tried their pastries so I was sure the quality is top notch. One thing that was hard to find is the location. Google Maps was telling me I had arrived but there was only a garage beside me. Sending this picture in case it's helpful for any other guests. They're located besides the facial place. They also have wifi so I was able to take a meeting from there.

reviewSample.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Perfect spot for coffee and work! ☕💻
Excellent coffee and a very welcoming atmosphere. The staff is friendly, and the WiFi is great, making it the perfect place to get some work done. Highly recommended if you're looking for quality coffee and a productive environment!

Black Sheep coffee around is a must go. We came with our toddler and dog and had a good time. There’s enough space inside to sit with your dog and they are pet friendly. The baby was able to move around. The food was fresh. And the coffee and Matcha are out of this world. If you’re in the area, I highly recommend you stop by.

I have been to Black Sheep Coffee several times, and it has become our family’s favorite coffee shop. The staff is patient, helpful, and kind. My wife and youngest son, who has autism, both love the coffee and the chocolate chip cookies.

The ambience is uplifting, the seating is cozy and comfortable, and the eclectic clientele is part of what makes having coffee in Coconut Grove such a delight. As a former Starbucks barista, I know what it is like on the other side of the counter, and what sets Black Sheep Coffee apart is the coffee, the service, and the genuine sense of community.

It feels like a true "third place," meaning a welcoming space outside home and work where people can gather, connect, and feel comfortable staying awhile. Sociologist Ray Oldenburg popularized that idea, and some 25 years ago he asked me to write a chapter in his book *Celebrating the Third Place*, so the concept has special meaning for me.

I am a disabled senior, and I was impressed enough to even download the app. I also appreciate that the facilities are ADA accessible. I use a walker and get about with ease, which makes the whole experience feel even more welcoming and inclusive.

The location is especially convenient if you take the train. You can hop off, grab a coffee, and be on your way. The general manager, Abner, is attentive and professional, and the place reminds me a bit of Uncommon Grounds, a coffee shop I used to visit daily in Berkeley. In my opinion, Black Sheep Coffee is one of the few places in South Florida that truly feels like a real neighborhood coffee shop.

reviewSample.text:
Cafe Grumpy is exactly the kind of coffee shop you want as a go to, simple, confident, and consistently done right. They use organic valley milk, the baristas are genuinely kind, and the whole place has that calm energy that makes you want to stay a little longer.

The coffee is the real reason though. They have single origin options and a light roast that tastes clean and bright, with zero bitterness, just smooth, intentional flavor. Great location, super friendly service, and the kind of spot that becomes part of your routine fast.

Liz and Alex were so incredibly nice ans patient with us as we wanted our coffee “for here” post order.

More on social media: @ratedbymaryam

Such a cute coffee shop. I got a vanilla matcha and it was yummyyy, as well as their avocado toast. they have some really cute posters on the wall & a good bit of seats, to sit down and catch up with a friend. street parking is easy to find.

Quaint spot! Loved it. Alex made a perfect latte & iced matcha. The gallery wall is a nice way to casually shop local art. The music volume was ideal and the playlist tasteful - a rarity in Miami. I will be working here again.
